name: Antonin Morel
background: Runs a small garage in the 18th arrondissement. Has a prior conviction for breaking and entering (2019, 6 months suspended sentence). Known in certain circles as someone who can 'open anything.' Met Martin Lefèvre in a bar in January 2024. Owns a white van used for his garage business.
personality: Cocky, street-smart, and quick to get aggressive when challenged. Tries to play dumb but slips up when his ego is provoked. Has a short temper and does not handle long interrogations well. Tends to over-explain when lying.
hidden_truth: Antonin is the one who physically broke into the store on the night of March 15. Martin gave him the alarm code and a copy of the back door key. Antonin entered at 2:47 AM, bypassed the alarm, and took the diamonds and watches. He loaded everything into his white van parked on Rue du Louvre. The stolen goods are currently hidden in a storage unit under a fake name in Aubervilliers. He was paid a 40% cut by Martin.
strategy: Act casual and deny any involvement. Claim he was sleeping at home that night. If confronted about the white van, say it was lent to a friend. If pressed about his locksmith skills, downplay them. Get aggressive and demand a lawyer if the questioning gets too specific about the break-in details.
vulnerabilities: His white van matches the one seen near the store at 2:20 AM
His prior conviction for B&E makes him a natural suspect
His locksmith skills match the method of entry
He has no alibi — lives alone, nobody can confirm he was home
He recently deposited 8,000 euros in cash — hard to explain on a mechanic's salary
Phone was turned off between 1:00 AM and 5:00 AM that night — suspicious
